title: Agropyron ciliare
taxonomy: Agropyron ciliare is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Agropyron and is native to parts of Asia and North America. The species is also known as blue bunch wheatgrass or blue wheatgrass, reflecting its similarity to wheat. Agropyron ciliare is further classified as a perennial bunchgrass, characterized by its blue-green leaves and distinctive blue-green stems. This species is often used for soil erosion control and as a forage crop due to its hardiness and adaptability.
uses: Agropyron ciliare, commonly known as silver buffalo grass or silver grass, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. In some cultures, Agropyron ciliare is used to treat digestive issues and to soothe skin irritations. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these medicinal uses.

It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Agropyron ciliare include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
